summary: Deprecated audit trail for the old Windows Update composite bundle. The official policy controls now live in a separate child record, while the pause timestamp writes remain documented here as historical behavior.

setting.name: Windows Update Disable Bundle
setting.casual_explanation: This bundle tries to stop Windows Update by combining policy-based switches with long-dated pause values.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it when they want to keep Windows Update from installing updates automatically or showing normal Windows Update access points.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Blocking Windows Update can stop unwanted updates, but it also removes a major source of security patches and feature fixes.
setting.professional_notes: Official Microsoft policy files document DisableWindowsUpdateAccess and NoAutoUpdate. The current app also writes PauseFeatureUpdatesEndTime, PauseQualityUpdatesEndTime, and PauseUpdatesExpiryTime under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WindowsUpdate\UX\Settings, which are runtime pause values rather than the main policy surface.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Update access is not blocked by this specific policy.

allowed_values.label: Remove access to Windows Update
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Update features and access points are removed by policy.

allowed_values.label: Automatic updating policy enabled
allowed_values.meaning: The Configure Automatic Updates policy is enabled and AUOptions determines behavior.

allowed_values.label: Automatic updates turned off
allowed_values.meaning: Automatic updates are prohibited by this policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes two official policy values and also writes three long-dated pause timestamps under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WindowsUpdate\UX\Settings. Those pause values are not the same thing as the official Group Policy controls.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems supporting these Windows Update policies

states.rationale: Leave the Windows Update access-removal policy unset.

windows_defaults.notes: This record intentionally does not validate the bundle as a whole because the app mixes policy values with runtime pause timestamps.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
intended_for: Systems that should keep receiving updates normally
avoid_for: Research-only documentation of the current app bundle

states.rationale: Do not apply the access-removal policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to avoid publishing a mixed policy-plus-runtime pause bundle as validated.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a reviewed Windows Update-off control regardless of how the app currently implements it.
tradeoffs: The current bundle remains documented but not validated.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ICM.admx mapping for DisableWindowsUpdateAccess
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps DisableWindowsUpdateAccess with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsUpdate.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file documents NoAutoUpdate under the AU policy key and shows the official Enable/Disable mapping for Configure Automatic Updates.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsUpdate.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains DisableWindowsUpdateAccess, NoAutoUpdate, and the driver-exclusion policy, and clarifies that NoAutoUpdate = 1 prohibits Windows from searching for updates.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableWindowsUpdateAccess = 1, NoAutoUpdate = 1, and three pause timestamp values under the UX settings path.

decision.why: The official policy values are real, but the current tweak mixes them with runtime pause timestamps in one over-broad bundle.
blocking_issues: The current tweak combines official policy values with non-policy pause timestamp writes under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WindowsUpdate\UX\Settings.
The bundle should be split into narrower controls before it is published as validated.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Local Microsoft ICM.admx mapping for DisableWindowsUpdateAccess: The local ADMX file maps DisableWindowsUpdateAccess with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.
validation_proof.notes: Backfilled from evidence_id local-icm-admx (official-doc); deprecated audit trail.
